---
title: Quarto extensions
---

Extensions, filters, and shortcodes add authoring behavior beyond basic
Quarto Markdown. They explain why syntax used in one course may not work in
another.

## Features seen in course repositories

| Feature | Example authoring pattern | Infrastructure required |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Browser-executed Python | A `pyodide` executable cell | Live/Pyodide extension and compatible HTML format |
| Hidden answer blocks | `.callout-solution` divs | Solution-rendering extension or filter |
| Learner/answer code filtering | `#| exercise: true`, `#| solution: true` | Profile-specific Lua filters |
| Download controls | A `download-button` shortcode | Repository-provided shortcode or extension |

## Contributor guidance

When editing ordinary course content:

- Reuse extension syntax already present in nearby source documents.
- Check the corresponding learner-facing and answer-bearing outputs where
  filtering is involved.
- Run project-provided validators for assets or scripts when relevant.
- Do not add or modify extensions simply to make a small content edit.

## Maintainer guidance

If a feature must be introduced or altered, inspect `_extensions/`, `filters/`,
profile files, output formats, and CI render commands as one change. A filter
can affect what is published even when a source document looks correct in
isolation.
